The vlogger wants to create a schedule that maximizes the number of unique sequences of visiting these sites. The number of different sequences is calculated by the factorial of the number of sites, which is 7! = 5040. This ensures each site is visited exactly once in a unique order.

The vlogger also plans to spend a specific amount of time at each site, following a normal distribution with a mean of 3 hours and a standard deviation of 0.5 hours. The probability that the total time spent visiting all 7 sites will exceed 22 hours is calculated using the normal distribution. The z-score for 22 hours is (22 - 21)/0.5 = 2, and the probability of exceeding 22 hours is the area to the right of z = 2, which is approximately 0.0228.
